originalTitle: South African leader warns anti-migrant protesters ahead of unofficial deadline
title: Let's Be Kind: A Message from South Africa!
content:
Hello, my friends!

Hi everyone! It's your friendly African news reporter here, ready to share a very important story with you. Today, we're going to talk about being kind and friendly to everyone, no matter where they come from. Our story comes from a beautiful country called South Africa. It's a land with amazing animals, tall mountains, and warm, sunny days.

Our Big African Family

Did you know that Africa is a super big continent? It's like a giant puzzle with many, many different countries. Each country is like a special house in our big African neighbourhood. We have neighbours like Zimbabwe, Mozambique, Nigeria, Kenya, and so many more! Just like you have friends who live next door, countries have friends too. We are all part of one big African family.

Moving Around is Normal

Sometimes, people move from one house to another, right? Maybe their family needs a bigger home, or their parents get a new job. It's the same for grown-ups in different countries. Sometimes, people move from one African country to another. They might move to find a new job, to be closer to family, or to find a safe place to live. This is called moving, or sometimes, when people move across countries, it's called migration. It's a very normal thing for people to do. Did you know? People have been moving and travelling across Africa for thousands of years! It's how new ideas and cultures spread.

Sometimes, Big Worries Happen

Recently, in South Africa, some people were talking about how people from other African countries should leave. They even said there was a special day, a 'deadline', when people should go home by. This made many people feel very sad and worried. Imagine if someone told your friend they couldn't play in your garden anymore, just because they lived in a different house! That wouldn't feel very kind, would it? It's important for everyone to feel welcome and safe, no matter where they are from.

Our Leader's Important Message

South Africa has a very important leader, like a principal for the whole country. He is called the President. The President saw that many people were feeling worried. So, he spoke out with a very important message. He said that everyone in South Africa should be kind and friendly to people from other countries. He warned everyone that it is not okay to make people feel unwelcome or to tell them they must leave. The President reminded everyone that South Africa is a home for all, and we should treat each other with love and respect. He wants everyone to live together happily and safely, like good neighbours should. He believes in being friendly. Did you know? A good leader always tries to make sure everyone in their country is safe, happy, and treated fairly, just like your teacher looks after everyone in your classroom!

What We Can All Do

So, what does this mean for you, my amazing young friends? It means we should always try to be kind. Kindness is like a superpower that makes everyone feel good. When you meet someone new, even if they speak a different language or come from a different place, try to be friendly. Smile, say hello, and maybe even share a game or a snack. Small acts of kindness can make a big difference. Remember, we are all part of one big human family, and especially one big African family. Let's always try to help each other and make everyone feel welcome. That's what true friendship is all about.

Being Friends Across Borders

People from different countries bring new ideas, delicious foods, and wonderful stories to share. This makes our communities richer and more fun! So, let's learn from our leaders and always choose to be kind. Let's make sure everyone feels safe and loved. It's important that we listen to messages of peace and friendship. When we are kind, we make our corner of Africa, and the whole world, a better place. Thank you for listening, and remember to always spread kindness wherever you go!
